aesthetic: The Iron Clock-Work — Carlylese letterpress drilled on a Prussian order-of-battle
anchor: The mid-Victorian six-volume letterpress folio of the History (Chapman & Hall, 1858-65), where Carlyle's capitalized Powers — Sham, Reality, Veracity, Fact, Dryasdust — stand on the page like Fraktur proper-nouns, crossed with the 18th-century Prussian order-of-battle plan and the drill-ground whose troops move 'straight, rigid, regular as iron clock-work... with the lightning sleeping in it'; the cadence is Old-Testament jeremiad heard 'surpassable only by Doomsday'.
rationale: The corpus's load-bearing fact is that the style IS the argument: a granite, deformed prose of parataxis, coined fire-compounds, and capitalized abstractions enacting a Sham-versus-Reality cosmology, in which a single Reality (Friedrich) is quarried out of and held against a 'swindler-century'. So the world is cold, iron, drilled, monumental — gunmetal and Prussian blue, the oxblood of the battle 'whirlwind of blood', the brass of the Pour le Merite, and one cold steel-spark of 'lightning'. High-contrast Didone display = the engraved Victorian title-page and the cold steel; sharp 2px corners = the drill. Never warm, never persuasive — compulsion, not consent.

fonts.display: Didot, 'Bodoni 72', 'Hoefler Text', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.body: Charter, 'Bitstream Charter', Cambria, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', Consolas, 'DejaVu Sans Mono', ui-monospace, monospace
